style="border-collapse:collapse;display:inline-table;margin-bottom:5pt;vertical-align:text-bottom;width:100.000%"><tr><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:23.335%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/><td style="width:1.0%"/><td style="width:74.465%"/><td style="width:0.1%"/></tr><tr><td colspan="3" style="background-color:#cceeff;border-top:1pt solid #000;padding:2px 1pt;text-align:left;vertical-align:top"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:9pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Reserves</span></td><td colspan="3" style="background-color:#cceeff;border-top:1pt solid #000;padding:2px 1pt;text-align:justify;vertical-align:top"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:9pt;font-weight:400;line-height:100%">Reserves are estimated remaining quantities of oil and natural gas and related substances anticipated to be economically producible, as of a given date, by application of development projects to known accumulations. In addition, there must exist, or there must be a reasonable expectation that there will exist, the legal right to produce or a revenue interest in the production, installed means of delivering oil and natural gas or related substances to the market and all permits and financing required to implement the project. Reserves should not be assigned to adjacent reservoirs isolated by major, potentially sealing, faults until those reservoirs are penetrated and evaluated as economically producible. Reserves should not be assigned to areas that are clearly separated from a known accumulation by a non-productive reservoir (i.e., absence of reservoir, structurally low reservoir or negative test results). Such areas may contain prospective resources (i.e., potentially recoverable resources from undiscovered accumulations).</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3" style="background-color:#ffffff;padding:2px porous and permeable underground formation containing a natural accumulation of producible natural gas and/or crude oil that is confined by impermeable rock or water barriers and is separate from All statements, other than statements of historical fact, including statements regarding our: future performance; business strategy; future operations (including drilling plans and capital plans); estimates and projections of revenues, losses, costs, expenses, returns, cash flow and financial position; reserve estimates and our ability to replace or increase reserves; anticipated benefits or other effects of strategic transactions (including the Double Eagle Acquisition and Viper&#8217;s Sitio Acquisition (in each case, as defined below) discussed in this report and other acquisitions or divestitures); and plans and objectives of management (including plans for future cash flow from operations and for executing environmental strategies) are forward-looking statements. Although we believe that the expectations and assumptions reflected in our forward-looking statements are reasonable as and when made, they involve risks and uncertainties that are difficult to predict and, in many cases, beyond our control. Subsidiaries </span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div id="i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_43"></div><div><span id="i3415f245f28740c4bd7c04450bb2c296"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">1.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:OrganizationConsolidationAndPresentationOfFinancialStatementsDisclosureTextBlock" id="f-334" continuedAt="f-334-1" escape="true">DESCRIPTION OF THE BUSINESS AND BASIS OF PRESENTATION</ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-334-1"><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i78477a99e6934fbd92de437464b96239"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Organization and Description of the Business</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Diamondback Energy, Inc., together with its subsidiaries (collectively referred to as &#8220;Diamondback,&#8221; the &#8220;Company,&#8221; &#8220;we&#8221; or &#8220;our&#8221; unless the context otherwise requires), is an independent oil and natural gas company currently focused on the acquisition, development, exploration and exploitation of unconventional, onshore oil and natural gas reserves primarily in the Permian Basin in West Texas.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As of March&#160;31, 2026, the wholly owned subsidiaries of Diamondback include Diamondback E&amp;P, a Delaware limited liability company, Rattler Midstream GP LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, Rattler Midstream LP, a Delaware limited partnership, QEP Resources, Inc., a Delaware corporation, Diamondback RE Holdco LLC, a Delaware limited liability company and Eclipse Merger Sub II, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:BasisOfAccountingPolicyPolicyTextBlock" id="f-335" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i2060c967903942298bb425596b28fdf7"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Basis of Presentation</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The condensed consolidated financial statements include the accounts of the Company and its subsidiaries, including its publicly-traded subsidiary, Viper Energy, Inc., after all significant intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated upon consolidation. As of March&#160;31, 2026, the Company is managed as <ix:nonFraction unitRef="segment" contextRef="c-1" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:NumberOfOperatingSegments" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-336"><ix:nonFraction unitRef="segment" contextRef="c-1" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:NumberOfReportableSegments" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-337">one</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> operating and reportable segment, the upstream segment, which is engaged in the acquisition, development, exploration and exploitation of unconventional, onshore oil and natural gas reserves primarily in the Permian Basin in West Texas and includes the activities of Viper as well as the Company&#8217;s remaining midstream operations.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On August&#160;19, 2025, upon completion of Viper&#8217;s Sitio Acquisition (as defined and discussed in Note 4&#8212;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_52">Acquisitions and Divestitures</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">), VNOM Sub, Inc., (formerly Viper Energy, Inc., &#8220;Former Viper&#8221;) became a wholly owned subsidiary of Viper Energy, Inc. (formerly New Cobra Pubco, Inc., &#8220;New Viper&#8221;).</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As of March&#160;31, 2026, the Company owned approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-55" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:MinorityInterestOwnershipPercentageByParent" scale="-2" id="f-338">39</ix:nonFraction>% of Viper&#8217;s combined outstanding Class A common stock and Class B common stock on a fully diluted basis, after giving effect to an option for certain Viper LLC equity holders to purchase and exchange up to <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-56" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="0" id="f-339">6,746,384</ix:nonFraction> of Class B common stock paired with an equivalent number of units representing limited liability company interests in Viper&#8217;s operating subsidiary (&#8220;Viper LLC Units&#8221;) into Viper Class A common stock. The Company determined that it controls the activities of Viper in accordance with the guidance for variable interest entities in Accounting Standards Codification (&#8220;ASC&#8221;) Topic 810, &#8220;Consolidation,&#8221; and therefore continues to consolidate Viper in the Company&#8217;s financial statements at March&#160;31, 2026. See further discussion of the Company&#8217;s determination that Viper is a variable interest entity (&#8220;VIE&#8221;) in Note 2&#8212;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_46">Summary of Significant Accounting Policies</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. The results of operations attributable to the non-controlling interest in Viper are presented within equity and net income and are shown separately from the equity and net income attributable to the Company.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">These condensed consolidated financial statements have been prepared by the Company without audit, pursuant to the rules and regulations of the SEC. They reflect all adjustments that are, in the opinion of management, necessary for a fair statement of the results for interim periods, on a basis consistent with the annual audited financial statements. All such adjustments are of a normal recurring nature. Certain information, accounting policies and footnote disclosures normally included in financial statements prepared in accordance with GAAP have been omitted pursuant to SEC rules and regulations, although the Company believes the disclosures are adequate to make the information presented not misleading. This Quarterly Report on Form 10&#8211;Q should be read in conjunction with the Company&#8217;s most recent </span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="https://www.sec.gov/ix?doc=/Archives/edgar/data/1539838/000153983826000010/fang-20251231.htm">Annual Report on Form 10&#8211;K</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, which contains a summary of the Company&#8217;s significant accounting policies and other disclosures.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:PriorPeriodReclassificationAdjustmentDescription" id="f-340" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i306ff407af514c26852575e296647e8a"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Reclassifications</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Certain prior period amounts have been reclassified to conform to the current period financial statement presentation. These reclassifications had an immaterial effect on the previously reported total assets, total liabilities, stockholders&#8217; equity, results of operations or cash flows.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">5</span></div></div></div><div id="i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_46"></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:67.5pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Diamondback Energy, Inc. and Subsidiaries</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements - (Continued)</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><div><span id="i00641f6f23434dd49a746d05d3e1ac66"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">2.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:SignificantAccountingPoliciesTextBlock" id="f-341" continuedAt="f-341-1" escape="true">SUMMARY OF SIGNIFICANT ACCOUNTING POLICIES</ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-341-1" continuedAt="f-341-2"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:UseOfEstimates" id="f-342" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i489d1088fc794a5080e46892a3c6eaf2"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Use of Estimates</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Certain amounts included in or affecting the Company&#8217;s condensed consolidated financial statements and related disclosures must be estimated by management, requiring certain assumptions to be made with respect to values or conditions that cannot be known with certainty at the time the condensed consolidated financial statements are prepared. These estimates and assumptions affect the amounts the Company reports for assets and liabilities and the Company&#8217;s disclosure of contingent liabilities as of the date of the condensed consolidated financial statements. Actual results could differ from those estimates.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Making accurate estimates and assumptions is particularly difficult in the oil and natural gas industry given the challenges resulting from volatility in oil and natural gas prices. For instance, geopolitical conflicts, elevated interest rates, effects of tariffs, actions taken by OPEC and its non-OPEC allies, global supply chain disruptions, measures to combat persistent inflation and instability in the financial sector have contributed to recent economic and pricing volatility. The financial results of companies in the oil and natural gas industry have been and may continue to be impacted materially as a result of these events and changing market conditions. Such circumstances generally increase uncertainty in the Company&#8217;s accounting estimates, particularly those involving financial forecasts.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company evaluates these estimates on an ongoing basis, using historical experience, consultation with experts and other methods the Company considers reasonable in the particular circumstances. Nevertheless, actual results may differ significantly from the Company&#8217;s estimates. Any effects on the Company&#8217;s business, financial position or results of operations resulting from revisions to these estimates are recorded in the period in which the facts that give rise to the revision become known. Significant items subject to such estimates and assumptions include estimates of proved oil and natural gas reserves and related present value estimates of future net cash flows therefrom, the carrying value of oil and natural gas properties, the fair value determination of assets acquired and liabilities assumed and estimates of income taxes, including deferred tax valuation allowances.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:ConsolidationVariableInterestEntityPolicy" id="f-343" continuedAt="f-343-1" escape="true"><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i6539658649d647eb925a4d2523bdb12d"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Variable Interest Entity</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Viper is a publicly traded corporation formed by the Company in 2014 to provide an attractive return to its stockholders (the largest of which is Diamondback) by focusing on business results, maximizing dividends through organic growth and pursuing accretive growth opportunities through acquisitions of mineral, royalty, overriding royalty, net profits and similar interests from the Company and from third parties. Viper has no employees and the Company provides management, operating and administrative services to Viper under a services and secondment agreement, including the services of the executive officers and other employees.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Viper meets the definition of a VIE under ASC Topic 810, &#8220;Consolidation,&#8221; and the Company continues to be the primary beneficiary of the VIE through its ability, via existing contractual agreements, to direct the activities that most significantly affect Viper&#8217;s economic performance. The Company also has the obligation to absorb losses and the right to receive benefits that could be significant to Viper. As such, the Company continues to consolidate the activity of Viper. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On March 4, 2026, Viper completed a secondary public offering with the Company and certain other Viper stockholders, along with J.P. Morgan Securities LLC and Goldman Sachs &amp; Co. LLC as underwriters (the &#8220;Underwriters&#8221;) (the &#8220;Secondary Offering&#8221;). The Company exchanged approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-57" decimals="-4" name="us-gaap:ConversionOfStockSharesConverted1" scale="6" id="f-344"><ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-58" decimals="-4" name="us-gaap:ConversionOfStockSharesConverted1" scale="6" id="f-345">12.39</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction> million shares of Viper Class B common stock and an equivalent number of Viper LLC Units for an equivalent number of shares of Viper Class A common stock and subsequently sold such shares in the Secondary Offering. On March 19, 2026, the Underwriters exercised an option to purchase approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-59" decimals="-4" name="us-gaap:SaleOfStockNumberOfSharesIssuedInTransaction" scale="6" id="f-346">0.51</ix:nonFraction> million additional shares of Viper Class A common stock from the Company for aggregate cash proceeds of approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-59" decimals="-6" name="fang:ProceedsFromSaleOfSubsidiaryCommonStock" scale="6" id="f-347">589</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million. The Company&#8217;s proceeds from the Secondary Offering were used for general corporate purposes and to accelerate debt reduction.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The 2025 Drop Down, the Sitio Acquisition (each as defined and discussed in Note 4&#8212;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_52">Acquisitions and Divestitures</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">) and the Secondary Offering were evaluated and determined not to be events that would cause the Company to change its conclusion regarding Viper&#8217;s status as a VIE, and the Company continues to be the primary beneficiary.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Viper maintains its own capital structure that is separate from the Company, and the Company is not under any obligation to provide additional financial support or investment to Viper. Viper&#8217;s assets cannot be used by the Company for general corporate purposes and the creditors of Viper&#8217;s liabilities do not have recourse to the Company&#8217;s assets. The assets and </span></div></ix:nonNumeric></ix:continuation><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">6</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:67.5pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Diamondback Energy, Inc. and Subsidiaries</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements - (Continued)</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-341-2"><ix:continuation id="f-343-1"><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">liabilities of Viper are included in the Company&#8217;s condensed consolidated balance sheets style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify"><span id="i35c1dbb110ce4b429b6a4e45c8c5f275"></span><span Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In November 2024, the FASB issued ASU 2024-03, &#8220;Income Statement&#8212;Reporting Comprehensive Income&#8212;Expense Disaggregation Disclosures (Subtopic 220-40) &#8211; Disaggregation of Income Statement Expenses,&#8221; which requires additional disclosure about specified categories of expenses included in relevant expense captions presented on the income statement. The amendments are effective for annual periods beginning after December 15, 2026, and for interim periods within fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2027. Early adoption is permitted. The amendments may be applied either prospectively or retrospectively. Management is currently evaluating this ASU to determine its impact on the Company&#8217;s disclosures. Adoption of the update will not impact the Company&#8217;s financial position, results of operations or liquidity.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company considers the applicability and impact of all ASUs. ASUs not listed above were assessed and determined to be either not applicable, previously disclosed, or not material upon adoption.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric></ix:continuation><div (Continued)</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-397-2"><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify"><span id="i3fba348c135a437484e7164dbb6475d9"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Double Eagle Acquisition</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On April 1, 2025, the Company completed its acquisition of all of the issued and outstanding interests of DE Permian, LLC, DE IV Combo, LLC and DE IV Operating, LLC, each of which were wholly owned subsidiaries of Double Eagle IV Midco, LLC (the &#8220;Double Eagle Acquisition&#8221;) for consideration of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-103" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:AssetAcquisitionConsiderationTransferred" scale="9" id="f-418">3.1</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion in cash and approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-104" decimals="-4" name="fang:AssetAcquisitionConsiderationTransferredEquityInterestShareIssuedNumberOfShares" scale="6" id="f-419">6.84</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock, including transaction costs and subject to customary post-closing adjustments. The assets acquired in the Double Eagle Acquisition consisted of approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="acre" contextRef="c-105" decimals="0" name="fang:AreaOfLandGross" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="0" id="f-420">67,700</ix:nonFraction> gross (<ix:nonFraction unitRef="acre" contextRef="c-105" decimals="0" name="us-gaap:AreaOfLand" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="0" id="f-421">40,000</ix:nonFraction> net) acres, which are primarily located in the Midland Basin and approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="acre" contextRef="c-106" decimals="0" name="fang:AreaOfLandGross" scale="0" id="f-422">407</ix:nonFraction> gross (<ix:nonFraction unitRef="acre" contextRef="c-106" decimals="0" name="us-gaap:AreaOfLand" scale="0" id="f-423">342</ix:nonFraction> net) horizontal locations in primary development targets. The Company funded the cash portion of the Double Eagle Acquisition through a combination of proceeds from the issuance of the $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-107" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromIssuanceOfLongTermDebt" scale="9" id="f-424">1.2</ix:nonFraction> billion aggregate principal amount of <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-108" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-425">5.550</ix:nonFraction>% Senior Notes due 2035 (the &#8220;2035 Notes&#8221;), proceeds from the 2025 Term Loan (as defined and discussed in Note 8&#8212;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_70">D</a><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_70">e</a><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_70">b</a><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_70">t</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">) and borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility. The Double Eagle Acquisition was accounted for as an asset acquisition in accordance with ASC Topic 805, &#8220;Business Combinations.&#8221;</span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify"><span id="ife61dbdb170d4da489bf82258d115f02"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Viper Acquisition</span></div><div style="text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify"><span id="i1fcdd29cebc548bb86a8923333d9eea7"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Sitio Acquisition</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On August&#160;19, 2025, Viper completed a series of transactions in which New Viper acquired Sitio, Sitio Royalties Operating Partnership, LP (&#8220;Sitio OpCo&#8221;) and their respective subsidiaries, pursuant to the Agreement and Plan of Merger, dated June 2, 2025, by and among Former Viper, Viper LLC, Sitio, Sitio OpCo, New Viper, Cobra Merger Sub, Inc. and Scorpion Merger Sub, Inc. (the &#8220;Sitio Acquisition&#8221;). The Sitio Acquisition was an all-equity transaction valued at approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-109" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:AssetAcquisitionConsiderationTransferredEquityInterestIssuedAndIssuable" scale="9" id="f-426">4.0</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion, including transaction costs and customary post-closing adjustments and the retirement of Sitio&#8217;s net debt of approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-110" decimals="-8" name="fang:AssetAcquisitionAcquireeRedemptionOfDebt" scale="9" id="f-427">1.2</ix:nonFraction>&#160;billion.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The mineral and royalty interests acquired in the Sitio Acquisition represent approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="acre" contextRef="c-111" decimals="0" name="fang:NetRoyaltyAcres" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="0" id="f-428">25,300</ix:nonFraction> net royalty acres in the Permian Basin and approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="acre" contextRef="c-112" decimals="0" name="fang:NetRoyaltyAcres" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="0" id="f-429">9,000</ix:nonFraction> net royalty acres in the Denver-Julesburg, Eagle Ford and Williston basins, for total acreage of approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="acre" contextRef="c-109" decimals="0" name="fang:NetRoyaltyAcres" format="ixt:num-dot-decimal" scale="0" id="f-430">34,300</ix:nonFraction> net royalty acres. See &#8220;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_52">&#8212;</a><span id="i0da02d5bb84c471696d5bff7c92a6e92"></span></span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_52">Viper Divestiture of Non-Permian Assets</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">&#8221; </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">above for discussion of the divestiture of Viper&#8217;s non-Permian acreage in the first quarter of 2026. Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Unevaluated properties not subject to depletion under full cost accounting.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Under the full cost method of accounting, the Company is required to perform a ceiling test each quarter which determines a limit, or ceiling, on the book value of proved oil and natural gas properties. The Company recorded a non-cash ceiling test impairment of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-1" decimals="-8" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfOilAndGasProperties" scale="9" id="f-451">1.4</ix:nonFraction> billion during the three months ended March&#160;31, 2026, which is included in the caption &#8220;Accumulated depletion, depreciation, amortization and impairment&#8221; on the condensed consolidated balance sheet. <ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-11" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfOilAndGasProperties" format="ixt:fixed-zero" scale="0" id="f-452">No</ix:nonFraction> impairment expense was recorded for the three months ended March&#160;31, 2025.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">9</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:67.5pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Diamondback Energy, Inc. and Subsidiaries</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements - (Continued)</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-431-2"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">In addition to commodity prices, the Company&#8217;s production rates, levels of proved reserves, future development costs, transfers of unevaluated properties and other factors will determine its actual ceiling test calculation and impairment analysis in future periods. If the future trailing <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="fang:OilAndGasPropertyAndEquipmentCommodityPricesTrailingPeriod" format="ixt-sec:durmonth" id="f-453">12</ix:nonNumeric>-month commodity prices decline as compared to the commodity prices used in prior quarters, the Company may have material write downs in subsequent quarters. It is possible that circumstances requiring additional impairment testing will occur in future interim periods, which could result in potentially material impairment charges being recorded.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div id="i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_64"></div><div><span Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(2)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;The long-term portion of the asset retirement obligation is included in the caption &#8220;Other long-term liabilities&#8221; in the Company&#8217;s condensed consolidated balance sheets.</span></div></ix:nonNumeric><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company&#8217;s asset retirement obligations primarily relate to the future plugging and abandonment of wells and related facilities. The Company estimates the future plugging and abandonment costs of wells, the ultimate productive life of the properties, a risk-adjusted discount rate and an inflation factor in order to determine the current present value of this obligation. To the extent future revisions to these assumptions impact the present value of the existing asset retirement obligation liability, a corresponding adjustment is made to the oil and natural gas property balance.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div id="i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_67"></div><div><span id="i922423920ddb4b38819263c26228f40f"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">7.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:RelatedPartyTransactionsDisclosureTextBlock" id="f-474" continuedAt="f-474-1" escape="true">RELATED PARTY TRANSACTIONS</ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-474-1" continuedAt="f-474-2"><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i3acc0abb2d3c4bb9b327618d39c42514"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Deep Blue</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company and Five Point Energy LLC have a joint venture, Deep Blue, in which the Company owned a <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-117" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:EquityMethodInvestmentOwnershipPercentage" scale="-2" id="f-475">30</ix:nonFraction>% equity ownership interest as of March&#160;31, 2026. The equity ownership interest is included in the caption &#8220;Other assets&#8221; on the Company&#8217;s consolidated balance sheets. Additionally, the Company has other related party transactions with Deep Blue in the ordinary course of business, which result in (i) certain accounts receivable from Deep Blue, (ii) accrued capital expenditures and other accrued payables related to a commitment to fund certain capital expenditures on projects that were in process at the time of the Deep Blue transaction, and (iii) lease operating expenses and capitalized expenses related to fees paid to Deep Blue under a <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-118" name="fang:AgreementTerm" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" As partial consideration for the Endeavor Acquisition, the Company issued <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-124" decimals="-4" name="us-gaap:BusinessAcquisitionEquityInterestsIssuedOrIssuableNumberOfSharesIssued" scale="6" id="f-492">117.27</ix:nonFraction> million shares, or <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-125" decimals="3" name="fang:OwnershipPercentageByOtherParties" scale="-2" id="f-493">39.8</ix:nonFraction>% of its then-outstanding common stock to the former owners of Endeavor (the &#8220;Endeavor equityholders&#8221;), the majority of which are currently held by SGF FANG Holdings, LP (&#8220;SGF&#8221;). Additionally, pursuant to a stockholders agreement executed with the Endeavor equityholders, the Endeavor equityholders have the right to propose for nomination between <ix:nonFraction unitRef="director" contextRef="c-126" decimals="INF" name="fang:StockholdersAgreementRightToProposeByCounterpartyNumberOfDirectors" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-494">one</ix:nonFraction> and <ix:nonFraction unitRef="director" contextRef="c-127" decimals="INF" name="fang:StockholdersAgreementRightToProposeByCounterpartyNumberOfDirectors" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-495">four</ix:nonFraction> directors for election to the Company&#8217;s board of directors as long as certain established ownership thresholds are maintained. As a result, SGF is considered a related party of the Company under ASC Topic 850 &#8220;Related Party Disclosures.&#8221;</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On November 28, 2025, the Company entered into a letter agreement with SGF, which provides SGF with the right, but not the obligation, to sell up to <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-128" decimals="-5" name="fang:ShareRepurchaseProgramNumberOfSharesCalledByRightOfCounterpartyToSell" scale="6" id="f-496">3.0</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock to the Company per quarter through December 31, 2026 at the most recent Nasdaq closing price of such transaction, pursuant to the letter agreement. Repurchases under the letter agreement are pursuant to the Company&#8217;s existing share repurchase program, and have been approved by the audit committee of the Company&#8217;s board of directors. Purchases under the repurchase program may be made from time to time in open market or privately negotiated transactions and are subject to market conditions, applicable regulatory and legal requirements, contractual obligations and other factors. The repurchase program does not require the Company to acquire any specific number of shares. This repurchase program may be suspended from time to time, modified, extended or discontinued by the board of directors at any time. During the three months ended March&#160;31, 2026, and 2025, the Company repurchased approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-1" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:StockRepurchasedDuringPeriodValue" scale="6" id="f-591">548</ix:nonFraction> million of common stock, which includes approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-18" decimals="-6" name="fang:StockRepurchasedAndRetiredDuringPeriodValueExcludingExciseTax" scale="6" id="f-592">509</ix:nonFraction> million for the repurchases from SGF, and $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-11" decimals="-6" name="fang:StockRepurchasedAndRetiredDuringPeriodValueExcludingExciseTax" scale="6" id="f-593">575</ix:nonFraction> million of common stock under the repurchase program, respectively, in each case excluding excise tax. For further discussion on the repurchases from SGF, see Note 7&#8212;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_67">Related Party Transactions</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Earnings (Loss) Per Share</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company&#8217;s earnings (loss) per share amounts have been computed using the two-class method. The two-class method is an earnings allocation proportional to the respective ownership among holders of common stock and participating securities. Basic earnings (loss) per share amounts have been computed based on the weighted-average number of shares of common stock outstanding for the period. Diluted earnings per share include the effect of potentially dilutive shares outstanding for the period, if any. Additionally, the per share earnings of Viper are included in the consolidated earnings per share computation based on the consolidated group&#8217;s holdings of the subsidiaries.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div of directors, the Company is authorized to issue up to <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-182" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" scale="6" id="f-627">11.8</ix:nonFraction> million shares of incentive and non-statutory stock options, restricted stock awards and restricted stock units, performance awards and stock appreciation rights to eligible employees. The Company currently has outstanding restricted stock units and performance-based restricted stock units under the Equity Plan. At March&#160;31, 2026, approximately <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-182" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:CommonStockCapitalSharesReservedForFutureIssuance" scale="6" id="f-628">3.0</ix:nonFraction> million shares of common stock remain available for future grants under the Equity Plan. The Company classifies its restricted stock units and performance-based restricted stock units as equity-based awards and estimates the fair values of restricted stock awards and units as the closing price of the Company&#8217;s common stock on the grant date of the award, which is expensed over the applicable vesting period.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-680-1" continuedAt="f-680-2"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span are recorded at fair value in the condensed consolidated balance sheet.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company has entered into multiple crude oil and natural gas derivatives, indexed to the respective indices as noted in the table below, to reduce price volatility associated with certain of its oil and natural gas sales. The Company has not designated its commodity derivative instruments as hedges for accounting purposes and, as a result, marks its commodity derivative instruments to fair value and recognizes the cash and non-cash changes in fair value in the condensed consolidated statements of operations under the caption &#8220;Gain (loss) on derivative instruments, net.&#8221;</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">By using derivative instruments to economically hedge exposure to changes in commodity prices, the Company exposes itself to credit risk and market risk. Credit risk is the failure of the counterparty to perform under the terms of the derivative contract. When the fair value of a derivative contract is positive, the counterparty owes the Company, which creates credit risk. The Company has entered into commodity derivative instruments only with counterparties that are also lenders under its credit facility and have been deemed an acceptable credit risk. As such, collateral is not required from either the counterparties or the Company on its outstanding commodity derivative contracts.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">17</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:67.5pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Diamondback Energy, Inc. and Subsidiaries</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements - (Continued)</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfDerivativeInstrumentsTextBlock" id="f-681" escape="true"><ix:continuation id="f-680-2" continuedAt="f-680-3"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As of March&#160;31, 2026, the Company had the following outstanding commodity derivative contracts. When aggregating multiple contracts, the weighted average contract price is disclosed.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div><table Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">(1)&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;The Company has fixed price basis swaps for the spread between the Cushing crude oil price and the Midland WTI crude oil price as well as the spread between the Henry Hub natural gas price, the Waha Hub and the HSC Hub natural gas price. The weighted average differential represents the amount of reduction to the Cushing, Oklahoma oil price and the Waha Hub and HSC Hub natural gas price for the notional volumes covered by the basis swap contracts.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div><table style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Interest Rate Swaps and Treasury Locks</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify"><span id="i395db762797347a789a9bbd665434b7c"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Interest Rate Swaps</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company had <ix:nonFraction unitRef="derivative" contextRef="c-251" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:DerivativeNumberOfInstrumentsHeld" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-761">two</ix:nonFraction> receive-fixed, pay-variable interest rate swap agreements for notional amounts of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-251" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:DerivativeNotionalAmount" scale="6" id="f-762">150</ix:nonFraction> million each, which were considered economic hedges of the Company&#8217;s <ix:nonFraction unitRef="number" contextRef="c-251" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:DerivativeFixedInterestRate" scale="-2" id="f-763">3.500</ix:nonFraction>% fixed rate senior notes due 2029. During the three months ended March&#160;31, 2026, the Company fully terminated and settled the remaining aggregate $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-252" decimals="-6" name="fang:DerivativeNotionalAmountTerminatedAndSettled" scale="6" id="f-764">300</ix:nonFraction> million notional amount of interest rate swaps for cash payments of approximately $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-252" decimals="-6" name="fang:PaymentsForDerivativeInstrumentsTerminatedAndSettled" scale="6" id="f-765">27</ix:nonFraction> million. The loss on the termination of interest rate swaps is recognized in the caption &#8220;Gain (loss) on derivative instruments, net&#8221; on the condensed consolidated statement of operations for the three months ended March&#160;31, 2026.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="if7a23fcaa50748f1b3812d1c5dda6d04"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Balance Sheet Offsetting of Derivative Assets and Liabilities</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The fair value of derivative instruments is generally determined using established index prices and other sources which are based upon, among other things, futures prices and time to maturity. These fair values are recorded by netting asset and liability positions, including any deferred premiums, that are with the same counterparty and are subject to contractual terms which provide for net settlement. See Note 13&#8212;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a Measurements</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> for further details.</span></div><div escape="true">FAIR VALUE MEASUREMENTS</ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-785-1" continuedAt="f-785-2"><div and Liabilities Measured at Fair Value on a Recurring Basis</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">As discussed in </span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New href="https://www.sec.gov/ix?doc=/Archives/edgar/data/1539838/000153983826000010/fang-20251231.htm#i27ba2a247b584e1aae74c0210baff60c_190">3</a><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Report on Form 10-K</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> for the year ended December 31, 2025, certain financial instruments of the Company are reported at fair value on the Company&#8217;s condensed consolidated balance sheets</span><span style="background-color:#ffffff;color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">. </span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The net amounts of derivative instruments are classified as current or noncurrent based on their anticipated settlement dates. The Company has an immaterial investment in the Class A common stock of Verde Clean Fuels, Inc., which is reported at fair value using observable, quoted stock prices and is included in &#8220;Other assets&#8221; on the Company&#8217;s condensed consolidated balance sheets at March&#160;31, 2026, and December&#160;31, 2025.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div style="bottom:0;position:absolute;width:100%"><div style="text-align:center;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">19</span></div></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"/><div style="min-height:67.5pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Diamondback Energy, Inc. and Subsidiaries</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements - (Continued)</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><ix:continuation id="f-785-2" continuedAt="f-785-3"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Viper LLC completed multiple acquisitions during 2024 with Tumbleweed Royalty IV, LLC, TWR IV SellCo Parent, LLC, Tumbleweed-Q Royalties, LLC, MC TWR Royalties, LP and MC TWR Intermediate, LLC. The terms of these acquisitions included provisions for contingent cash consideration based on the average price of WTI sweet crude oil prompt month futures contracts for the calendar year 2025 (the &#8220;2026 WTI Contingent Liability&#8221;), which resulted in an aggregate payment of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-261" decimals="-6" name="fang:AssetAcquisitionContingentConsiderationPaid" scale="6" id="f-786">20</ix:nonFraction> million in January 2026. The 2026 WTI Contingent Liability was reported at fair value using observable market data inputs and a Monte Carlo pricing model, which are considered Level 2 inputs within the fair value hierarchy.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:OffsettingAssetsTableTextBlock" id="f-789" Viper Revolving Credit Facility, the 2025 Term Loan and Viper 2025 Term Loan (prior to repayment and termination) approximate their carrying values based on borrowing rates available to the Company for bank loans with similar terms and maturities and are classified as Level 2 in the fair value hierarchy. The fair values of the outstanding notes were determined using the quoted market price at each period end, a Level 1 classification in the fair value hierarchy.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i2ba31c807ee44febafafbd948c743c6b"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Assets and Liabilities Measured at Fair Value on a Nonrecurring Basis</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Certain assets and liabilities are measured at fair value on a nonrecurring basis in certain circumstances. These assets and liabilities can include those acquired in a business combination, inventory, proved and unproved oil and natural gas properties, equity method investments, asset retirement obligations and other long-lived assets that are written down to fair value when impaired or held for sale. style="min-height:67.5pt;width:100%"><div><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:8pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_7">Table of Contents</a></span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Diamondback Energy, Inc. and Subsidiaries</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements - (Continued)</span></div><div style="text-align:center"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">(Unaudited)</span></div></div><div><span id="i98512dff309e434a8efded350ceecbf0"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">15.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:CommitmentsAndContingenciesDisclosureTextBlock" id="f-885" continuedAt="f-885-1" escape="true">COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES</ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-885-1"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company is a party to various routine legal proceedings, disputes and claims arising in the ordinary course of its business, including those that arise from interpretation of federal and state laws and regulations affecting the crude oil and natural gas industry, personal injury claims, title disputes, royalty disputes, contract claims, employment claims, claims alleging violations of antitrust laws, contamination claims relating to oil and natural gas exploration and development and environmental claims, including claims involving assets previously sold to third parties and no longer part of the Company&#8217;s current operations. While the ultimate outcome of the pending proceedings, disputes or claims and any resulting impact on the Company, cannot be predicted with certainty, the Company&#8217;s management believes that none of these matters, if ultimately decided adversely, will have a material adverse effect on the Company&#8217;s financial condition, results of operations or cash flows. The Company&#8217;s assessment is based on information known about the pending matters and its experience in contesting, litigating and settling similar matters. Actual outcomes could differ materially from the Company&#8217;s assessment. The Company records accrued liabilities for contingencies related to outstanding legal proceedings, disputes or claims when information available indicates that a loss is probable and the amount of the loss can be reasonably estimated.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="i34db3b7298884a78ad8450df7cb03fc9"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Environmental Matters</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The United States Department of the Interior, Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement, ordered several oil and gas operators, including a corporate predecessor of Energen Corporation, to perform decommissioning and reclamation activities related to a Louisiana offshore oil and gas production platform and related facilities. In response to the insolvency of the operator of record, the government ordered the former operators and/or alleged former lease record title owners to decommission the platform and related facilities. The Company has agreed to an arrangement with other operators to contribute to a trust to fund the decommissioning costs, however, the Company&#8217;s portion of such costs are not expected to be material.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">Several coastal Louisiana parishes and the State of Louisiana have filed numerous lawsuits under Louisiana&#8217;s State and Local Coastal Resources Management Act (&#8220;SLCRMA&#8221;) against numerous oil and gas producers seeking damages for coastal erosion in or near oil fields located within Louisiana&#8217;s coastal zone. The Company is a defendant in <ix:nonFraction unitRef="lawsuit" contextRef="c-322" decimals="INF" name="fang:NumberOfLawsuitsCompanyIsDefendant" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-886">five</ix:nonFraction> of these cases. The Company has exercised contractual indemnification rights where applicable. Plaintiffs&#8217; SLCRMA theories are unprecedented and there remains significant uncertainty about the claims (both as to scope and damages). Although the Company cannot predict the ultimate outcome of these matters, the Company believes the claims lack merit and intends to continue vigorously defending these lawsuits.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div id="i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_94"></div><div><span id="i9e98a4db85c4442897fc6cab2f77ecab"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">16.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="c-1" name="us-gaap:SubsequentEventsTextBlock" id="f-887" continuedAt="f-887-1" escape="true">SUBSEQUENT EVENTS</ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-887-1" continuedAt="f-887-2"><div><span id="ic8e46c3bf9bc44e6bec2b033f7518c5f"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Pending Viper Riverbend Acquisition</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On May 1, 2026, Viper entered into a definitive purchase and sale agreement to acquire all of the equity interests of Riverbend Oil &amp; Gas IX, L.L.C., an entity owning certain mineral and royalty interests, from Riverbend Oil &amp; Gas IX (AIV), L.L.C. and ROG IX, L.L.C. (collectively, &#8220;Riverbend&#8221;) for consideration consisting of approximately (i) $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usd" contextRef="c-323" decimals="-6" name="us-gaap:AssetAcquisitionPriceOfAcquisitionExpected" scale="6" id="f-888">337</ix:nonFraction>&#160;million in cash and (ii) <ix:nonFraction unitRef="shares" contextRef="c-324" decimals="-4" name="fang:AssetAcquisitionConsiderationTransferredEquityInterestIssuedAndIssuableShares" scale="6" id="f-889">3.69</ix:nonFraction> million shares of Viper&#8217;s Class A Common Stock, in each case, subject to customary closing adjustments (the pending &#8220;Viper Riverbend Acquisition&#8221;). The pending Viper Riverbend Acquisition is expected to close during the third quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. </span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="ie88d031fad1f40b080db861b9417182d"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">First Quarter 2026 Dividend Declaration</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">On April&#160;29, 2026, the board of directors of the Company approved an increase in the Company&#8217;s annual base dividend to $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerShare" contextRef="c-325" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:CommonStockDividendsPerShareDeclared" scale="0" id="f-890">4.40</ix:nonFraction> per share of common stock and declared a base cash dividend for the first quarter of 2026 of $<ix:nonFraction unitRef="usdPerShare" contextRef="c-326" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:CommonStockDividendsPerShareDeclared" scale="0" id="f-891">1.10</ix:nonFraction> per share of common stock, payable on May&#160;21, 2026, to its stockholders of record at the close of business on May&#160;14, 2026. Future dividends are at the discretion of the Company&#8217;s board of directors.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify"><span id="ic89d8ebdbfa748d2b2bdbca84f9eead5"></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:700;line-height:120%">Repayments of Debt</span></div><div continuedAt="f-900-1" escape="true">SEGMENT INFORMATION</ix:nonNumeric></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><ix:continuation id="f-900-1"><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Company is managed on a consolidated basis as <ix:nonFraction unitRef="segment" contextRef="c-1" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:NumberOfOperatingSegments" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" id="f-901">one</ix:nonFraction> operating segment and <ix:nonFraction unitRef="segment" contextRef="c-1" decimals="INF" name="us-gaap:NumberOfReportableSegments" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" scale="0" id="f-902">one</ix:nonFraction> reportable segment, the upstream segment, which is engaged in the acquisition, development, exploration and exploitation of unconventional, onshore oil and natural gas reserves primarily in the Permian Basin in West Texas. This singular operating and reportable segment is comprised of (i) the Company and its wholly owned subsidiaries, and (ii) Viper and its consolidated subsidiaries, which have been aggregated due to the similarity in their economic characteristics, products and services, processes, type of customers, method of distribution for their products and the regulatory environment in which they operate. The upstream segment derives its revenue from customers through the sale of oil and natural gas products as well as other immaterial service contracts. See Note 3&#8212;</span><span style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline"><a style="color:#0000ff;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%;text-decoration:underline" href="#i64226f89bc01470fb2c113ff5ce9a825_49">Revenue from Contracts with Customers</a></span><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%"> for further discussion of the Company&#8217;s sources of revenue.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The Chief Operating Decision Maker (&#8220;CODM&#8221;), a senior executive committee that is comprised of the Company&#8217;s Chief Executive Officer, Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer, uses the Company&#8217;s condensed consolidated financial results to make key operating decisions, assess performance and to allocate resources. The measures of segment profit or loss and total assets utilized by the CODM are net income and total assets as reported on the condensed consolidated statements of operations and the condensed consolidated balance sheets, respectively. The significant expense categories, their amounts and other segment items that are regularly provided to the CODM are those that are reported in the Company&#8217;s condensed consolidated statements of operations.</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span style="color:#000000;font-family:'Times New Roman',serif;font-size:10pt;font-weight:400;line-height:120%">The CODM uses consolidated net income as a measure of profitability to evaluate segment performance and to make capital allocation decisions such as reinvestment in the business or return of capital through the payment of base and variable dividends or repurchases under the share repurchase program.</span></div></ix:continuation><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="height:36pt;position:relative;width:100%"><div Item 1A. Developments</span></div><div style="text-align:justify;text-indent:36pt"><span><br/></span></div><div style="padding-left:36pt;text-align:justify"><span
